channel.title: International Organisation Jobs (Brussels/NATO)
channel.description: Job listings for International Organisations roles, scraped by Pol-Intel.

item.title: Associate Project Officer - Western Balkans, Albania
item.description: Context The International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance (International IDEA) is an intergovernmental organization composed of 35 member states that supports democracy worldwide. Headquartered in Stockholm and with field offices in 18 countries, it promotes sustainable democratic change by providing comparative knowledge, assisting in democratic reform, and influencing policies and politics. The Institute’s key impact areas are: electoral processes, digitalization and democracy, constitution-building, democracy assessments, as well as political participation and representation. International IDEA supports democratic change through its convening potential at the country, expert, and international levels. It works in the spirit of peer dialogue, nurturing an open and pluralistic understanding of democracy that is respectful of the national context. Based in Brussels, International IDEA’s Regional Europe Programme (REO) implements democracy assistance projects in young democracies across Europe and liaises with European Union institutions and Member States. With the support of its field offices in Albania, Moldova and Ukraine, the Institute supports extensively the integrity of elections in EU enlargement countries, helping national institutions to address emerging issues of digitalization and democracy, cybersecurity, foreign interference and malign information, online campaigning and impact of AI, and liaising them with EU institutions. International IDEA expands its engagement through the second phase of the project “Closing the Digital Gap on Elections in EU Accession”. Building on the Institute’s networks among electoral authorities across the EU candidate and potential candidate countries and EU policymakers in Brussels, the project seeks to foster dialogue and exchanges between stakeholders across the Western Balkans and Eastern Europe—including electoral authorities, policymakers, experts and civil society actors—and relevant EU institutions. It does so through a combination of consultative mapping exercises, research, peer exchanges, capacity-building activities, and policy dialogues in Brussels and across the EU enlargement region. The Associate Project Officer (APO) will support the planning and implementation of project activities, in close cooperation with the Tirana-based Project Coordinator/Officer, and under the supervision of the Brussels-based Programme Manager. They will support the planning, design, implementation, communication, and reporting of relevant project activities. Duties and Responsibilities Supports the implementation of project initiatives and activities, contributing to work planning, operational coordination and the timely delivery of project outputs; Conducts background research, stakeholder mapping and information gathering to support project design, implementation and decision-making; Collaborates closely with project team members in Brussels and Tirana to coordinate activities, share information and ensure consistency across project components; Supports engagement with electoral authorities, civil society organisations, EU institutions, consultants and service providers, facilitating communication and follow-up on project activities and partnerships; Assists in the organisation and delivery of project events, workshops, trainings and study visits, including participant coordination, agenda development, logistical arrangements and post-event follow-up; Contributes to the implementation of communication and outreach activities, including the preparation of content for digital platforms, publications and stakeholder engagement initiatives; Collects, organises and analyses project information, including activity data, stakeholder feedback, testimonials and communications materials, contributing to project reporting and documentation; Supports the development of knowledge products and research outputs by conducting desk research, drafting background materials, reviewing content and facilitating dissemination activities; Maintains stakeholder databases and supports the establishment and maintenance of professional networks and partnerships relevant to the project's objectives; Contributes to monitoring, evaluation and learning processes through data collection, documentation of results and lessons learned, and support to evidence-based reporting in line with project and donor requirements. General Profile Requires good knowledge, excellent skills and experience in the field of democracy assistance; Will contribute to different aspects of the project life cycle, and will support the projects vis-à-vis all internal and external stakeholders; Adds value to team-based activities in their unit; collaborates with other entities of the Institute as required; Can be expected to travel to any geographical area involved in their projects (Brussels and EU enlargement countries); Follows internal procedures to ensure high standards of performance and compliance with Institutional guidelines; Acts in accordance with International IDEA’s core values; Respect, Integrity and Professionalism; Integrates gender equality, diversity, and social inclusion (GEDSI) considerations into all tasks and activities, in alignment with the Institute’s Core Values and commitment to the GEDSI policy. Reporting line Programme Manager – Western Balkans; Education and Experience University degree in political science, social science, law, international development or other relevant discipline; A minimum of three (3) years of relevant professional experience in democracy promotion, digitalization, the EU digital acquis (Digital Services Act, Artificial Intelligence Act, the European Media Freedom Act, and Network and Information Service Directive). and its impact on democracy and electoral processes. , supporting project implementation Experience supporting project implementation, including coordination, administrative tasks, reporting, and documentation of project activities and outputs; Demonstrated experience coordinating project activities, including workshops, trainings, meetings, and stakeholder events Experience engaging with a range of stakeholders (public institutions, civil society organisations, and project partners) is an advantage; Experience in conducting qualitative comparative research, data collection, and analysis is considered an advantage; Ability to prepare or contribute to written outputs, such as reports, briefing notes, meeting summaries, and project documentation; Fluency in English is required; Knowledge of Bosnian-Croatian-Montenegrin-Serbian (BCMS), or other languages of the Western Balkans constitutes an asset. Terms of Contract: Local Post. Fixed term appointment for two (2) years Remuneration - ALL 265 417 gross per month Please note as this is a local post, International IDEA will bear no costs relating to the relocation of the selected candidate to the duty station. This position is open to those legally authorized to live and work in Albania for the duration of the contract. International IDEA is not able to support work permit applications. Use of AI in Recruitment International IDEA places strong emphasis on human input in recruitment. Candidates must disclose any use of AI tools. All application materials, written assessments, and interview responses (as applicable) must be entirely human-generated and reflect the candidate’s own knowledge, skills, and experience. The use of AI to generate content or misrepresent qualifications is not permitted. AI‑powered assistive technologies (such as screen readers or speech‑to‑text tools) may be used for accessibility purposes to ensure equal access to the recruitment process. Applying for this position: Applications should be submitted online no later than 23:59 (CEST) 19 July 2026. Please note that all applications must be made in English. Selection Process: Evaluation of qualified candidates will include a written test assessment and up to two (2) interviews. Candidates selected to proceed to the next stage of the process will be contacted once all applications have been carefully reviewed. All candidates will be notified of the status of their application via email once the process has been finalized. Due to the anticipated volume of applications, International IDEA will review applications on a rolling basis, and the vacancy may close earlier than the advertised deadline. Applicants are therefore encouraged to apply early. International IDEA is an equal-opportunity employer which seeks to further diversify its staff in terms of gender, culture and nationality.
item.dc:creator: International IDEA

item.title: Project Coordinator, Albania
item.description: Context The International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance (International IDEA) is an intergovernmental organization composed of 35 member states that supports democracy worldwide. Headquartered in Stockholm and with field offices in 18 countries, it promotes sustainable democratic change by providing comparative knowledge, assisting in democratic reform, and influencing policies and politics. The Institute’s key impact areas are: electoral processes, digitalization and democracy, constitution-building, democracy assessments, as well as political participation and representation. International IDEA supports democratic change through its convening potential at the country, expert, and international levels. It works in the spirit of peer dialogue, nurturing an open and pluralistic understanding of democracy that is respectful of the national context. Based in Brussels, International IDEA’s Regional Europe Programme (REP) implements democracy assistance projects in young democracies across Europe and liaises with European Union institutions and Member States. With the support of its field offices in Albania, Moldova and Ukraine, the Institute supports extensively the integrity of elections in EU enlargement countries, helping national institutions to address emerging issues of digitalization and democracy, cybersecurity, foreign interference and malign information, online campaigning and impact of AI, and liaising them with EU institutions. International IDEA expands its engagement through the second phase of the project “Closing the Digital Gap on Elections in EU Accession”. Building on the Institute’s networks among electoral authorities across the EU candidate and potential candidate countries and EU policymakers in Brussels, the project seeks to foster dialogue and exchanges between stakeholders across the Western Balkans and Eastern Europe — including electoral authorities, policymakers, experts and civil society actors—and relevant EU institutions. It does so through a combination of consultative mapping exercises, research, peer exchanges, capacity-building activities, and policy dialogues in Brussels and across the EU enlargement region. The Project Coordinator will be responsible for implementing project activities, in close cooperation with the Associate Project Officer and the Brussels – based Programme Manager. They will coordinate and support the planning, design, implementation, communication, and reporting of relevant project activities. The Project Coordinator also represents International IDEA in meetings and events as delegated. Duties and Responsibilities Coordinates the implementation of project initiatives and activities, ensuring alignment with agreed objectives; Actively contributes to project planning and decision-making through substantive inputs in meetings and technical discussions.; Delivers thematic interventions at conferences and events. Represents the project in external engagements, as delegated by the line manager; Manages the organization of events, managing substantive content development, logistical planning, and administrative processes to ensure high-quality delivery; Contributes to the Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) component of the project, ensuring compliance with International IDEA’s results framework and contributing to evidence-based reporting and learning; Monitors project expenditure, and supports grant management processes, ensuring compliance with donor requirements and internal policies; Develops and maintains relevant professional contacts with the respective electoral authorities, civil society, and other key stakeholders invested in strengthening election standards across EU enlargement countries; Manages the engagement of consultants , develops contractual documentation, and tracks the quality of deliverables, in line with International IDEA’s policies and procedures; Conducts ongoing analysis of trends and developments in digitalisation and democracy within EU candidate and potential candidate countries, producing insights to inform project design and implementation; Collaborates closely with relevant International IDEA colleagues in Brussels and Stockholm as well as with the donor. General Profile Combines in-depth knowledge of democratic development, acquired through excellent skills including project management skills, communication skills and relevant experience in their field; Will contribute significantly to assigned projects all along the project life cycle, i.e. from project design and funding to project assessment; will lead meetings and discussions, supervise publications and review input from external providers; Has contributed to publications on the relevant subject; is recognized as a knowledge resource in their broad professional community; Can work with minimal guidance and supervision; Adds value to team-based activities in their team; collaborates with other entities of the Institute; Acts as a model or mentor for less experienced colleagues; Can be expected to travel regionally to any geographical area involved in their project; Follows internal procedures to ensure high standards of performance and compliance with Institutional guidelines; Acts in accordance with International IDEA’ s core values; Respect, Integrity and Professionalism; Integrates gender equality, diversity, and social inclusion (GEDSI) considerations into all tasks and activities, in alignment with the Institute’s Core Values and commitment to the GEDSI policy. Reporting line Programme Manager, Western Balkans Education and Experience University degree in political science, law, international relations, public administration or other related disciplines; Minimum of five (5) years of relevant experience in the implementation of projects in the field of democracy and governance support; Knowledge of democratic development and democracy assistance in at least one of the countries of the Western Balkans; Knowledge on digitalization policies, the EU digital acquis (Digital Services Act, Artificial Intelligence Act, the European Media Freedom Act, and Network and Information Service Directive). and its impact on democracy and electoral processes. Experience in one of the following areas: electoral assistance, political participation and representation); Good knowledge of the political context in the EU candidate and potential candidate countries, in particular of electoral cycles; Knowledge of issues related to digitalization and democracy, electoral integrity, online campaigning, information integrity, cybersecurity, AI, or related fields; Experience with liaising with government and independent institutions, parliament, political parties, civil society and diplomatic missions; Experience organizing and coordinating international conferences, workshops, training activities, or multi-stakeholder events; Experience mainstreaming gender in project activities. Fluency in written and oral Albanian and English required. Terms of Contract: Local Post. Fixed term appointment for two (2) years Remuneration - ALL 391 417 Gross per month Please note as this is a local post, International IDEA will bear no costs relating to the relocation of the selected candidate to the duty station. This position is open to those legally authorized to live and work in Albania for the duration of the contract. International IDEA is not able to support work permit applications. Use of AI in Recruitment International IDEA places strong emphasis on human input in recruitment. Candidates must disclose any use of AI tools. All application materials, written assessments, and interview responses (as applicable) must be entirely human-generated and reflect the candidate’s own knowledge, skills, and experience. The use of AI to generate content or misrepresent qualifications is not permitted. AI‑powered assistive technologies (such as screen readers or speech‑to‑text tools) may be used for accessibility purposes to ensure equal access to the recruitment process. Applying for this position: Applications should be submitted online no later than 23:59 (CEST) 19 July 2026. Please note that all applications must be made in English. Selection Process: Evaluation of qualified candidates will include a written test assessment and up to two (2) interviews. Candidates selected to proceed to the next stage of the process will be contacted once all applications have been carefully reviewed. All candidates will be notified of the status of their application via email once the process has been finalized. Due to the anticipated volume of applications, International IDEA will review applications on a rolling basis, and the vacancy may close earlier than the advertised deadline. Applicants are therefore encouraged to apply early. International IDEA is an equal-opportunity employer which seeks to further diversify its staff in terms of gender, culture and nationality.
item.dc:creator: International IDEA

item.title: Procurement Officer, Ukraine
item.description: Context The International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance (International IDEA) is an intergovernmental organization of 35 member states, with a mission to support sustainable democracy worldwide through policy-relevant knowledge, capacity development, advocacy, and the convening of dialogues. The Institute pursues this mission by developing, sharing and enabling the use of comparative knowledge to reform-makers, combining research, global knowledge tools, and technical assistance to support democratic reform and influence policies and politics at the national and global level. The Regional Europe Programme (REP) has a strategic objective to support democracy in Europe, with an emphasis on Eastern Europe and the Western Balkans. In Ukraine, International IDEA aims to support state and civil society organizations in enhancing the resilience and quality of democratic institutions and processes. Key areas of our engagement relate to electoral processes and political finance, parliamentary strengthening, and promoting the expertise and capacity of civil society organizations to participate in decision-making processes. International IDEA programming in Ukraine has expanded significantly. The Programme’s key areas of focus are provision of support to parliamentary strengthening and to enhancing electoral integrity; other areas of work may include activities to foster greater civic engagement in democratic processes, support to strengthening the culture of constitutionalism, digitalization, promotion of gender equality and women’s participation in political and public life. The Procurement Officer is responsible for managing and overseeing the procurement and contract management function. The role ensures that procurement activities are conducted in a transparent, efficient, and compliant manner, aligned with organizational policies, procedures and best value principles throughout the procurement cycle. The Procurement Officer also provides advisory support, strengthens procurement systems, and contributes to continuous improvement initiatives. Duties and Responsibilities Procurement and Contract Management Plan, manage, and coordinate procurement and contracting activities across the organization; Review and analyze procurement requisitions to ensure completeness, accuracy, and compliance with requirements; Lead sourcing processes including tendering, evaluation, negotiation, and contract award; Draft, review, and manage contracts, agreements, and other procurement-related documentation; Provide technical support on complex and high-value procurement cases; Ensure proper contract administration, including performance monitoring and compliance with contractual obligations. Advisory and Compliance Provide advice, guidance, and interpretation to staff on procurement policies and procedures; Ensure that procurement and contracting activities comply with IDEA policies and procedures, donor requirements, and applicable regulations; Conduct procurement compliance reviews and identify areas of non-compliance or risk; Support audit processes and respond to audit queries related to procurement; Promote ethical procurement practices and uphold transparency and accountability standards. Policy Development and Process Improvement Analyze procurement policies and procedures to identify gaps and areas for improvement; Develop and implement updated guidelines, tools, and standard operating procedures; Contribute to the development of procurement strategy and planning frameworks; Promote the application of best practices in procurement management. Reporting and Analysis Prepare procurement and contract management reports for management; Conduct data analysis, benchmarking, and reporting to support decision-making; Maintain accurate procurement records and documentation systems. Capacity Building and Knowledge Sharing Provide training and capacity-building support to staff on procurement policies, procedures, and best practices; Develop training materials, tools, and knowledge resources; Support organizational learning and knowledge-sharing initiatives. Stakeholder Management Liaise with internal stakeholders, suppliers, and service providers; Facilitate effective communication to resolve procurement-related issues; Build and maintain professional relationships with vendors and partners. Risk Management Identify and assess procurement risks and implement mitigation measures; Ensure integrity and accountability in procurement processes; Contribute to organizational risk management frameworks as they relate to procurement. General Profile Requires in-depth knowledge, excellent skills and relevant experience in the field of procurement management, purchasing and tendering; Can work with minimal guidance and supervision; Adds value to team-based activities in their unit; collaborates with other entities of the Institute; Acts as a model and mentor for less experienced colleagues; May be expected to travel globally to any geographical area involved in their projects; Follows internal procedures to ensure high standards of performance and compliance with Institutional guidelines; Acts in accordance with International IDEA’ s core values; Respect, Integrity and Professionalism; Integrates gender equality, diversity, and social inclusion (GEDSI) considerations into all tasks and activities, in alignment with the Institute’s Core Values and commitment to the GEDSI policy. Reporting line Ukraine Programme Lead. Education and Experience University degree in Procurement, Business Administration, Accounting, Economics, Finance, or a related discipline; Professional certification in procurement or finance (e.g. CIPS, ACCA) is an advantage; Minimum of five (5) years of relevant professional experience in procurement management; Demonstrated experience in procurement policy implementation and contract management applied in international organizations; Experience in an international or multi-stakeholder environment is an advantage. Excellent knowledge in written and oral English and Ukrainian is required; Terms of Contract: Local Post. Fixed term appointment until 31 December 2027. Remuneration - USD 3 418 gross per month Please note as this is a local post, International IDEA will bear no costs relating to the relocation of the selected candidate to the duty station. This position is open to those legally authorized to live and work in Ukraine for the duration of the contract. International IDEA is not able to support work permit applications. Use of AI in Recruitment International IDEA places strong emphasis on human input in recruitment. Candidates must disclose any use of AI tools. All application materials, written assessments, and interview responses (as applicable) must be entirely human-generated and reflect the candidate’s own knowledge, skills, and experience. The use of AI to generate content or misrepresent qualifications is not permitted. AI‑powered assistive technologies (such as screen readers or speech‑to‑text tools) may be used for accessibility purposes to ensure equal access to the recruitment process. Applying for this position: Applications should be submitted online no later than 23:59 (CEST) 22nd July 2026. Please note that all applications must be made in English. Selection Process: Evaluation of qualified candidates will include a written test assessment and up to two (2) interviews. Candidates selected to proceed to the next stage of the process will be contacted once all applications have been carefully reviewed. All candidates will be notified of the status of their application via email once the process has been finalized. Due to the anticipated volume of applications, International IDEA will review applications on a rolling basis, and the vacancy may close earlier than the advertised deadline. Applicants are therefore encouraged to apply early. International IDEA is an equal-opportunity employer which seeks to further diversify its staff in terms of gender, culture and nationality.
item.dc:creator: International IDEA

item.title: Project Coordinator, Ukraine
item.description: Context The International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance (International IDEA) is an intergovernmental organization of 35 member states, with a mission to support sustainable democracy worldwide through policy-relevant knowledge, capacity development, advocacy, and the convening of dialogues. The Institute pursues this mission by developing, sharing and enabling the use of comparative knowledge to reform-makers, combining research, global knowledge tools, and technical assistance to support democratic reform and influence policies and politics at the national and global level. The Regional Europe Programme (REP) has a strategic objective to support democracy in Europe, with an emphasis on Eastern Europe and the Western Balkans. In Ukraine, International IDEA aims to support state and civil society organizations in enhancing the resilience and quality of democratic institutions and processes. Key areas of our engagement relate to electoral processes and political finance, parliamentary strengthening, and promoting the expertise and capacity of civil society organizations to participate in decision-making processes. International IDEA programming in Ukraine has expanded significantly. The Programme’s key areas of focus are provision of support to parliamentary strengthening and to enhancing electoral integrity; other areas of work may include activities to foster greater civic engagement in democratic processes, support to strengthening the culture of constitutionalism, digitalization, promotion of gender equality and women’s participation in political and public life. The Project Coordinator support the coordination of project activities, stakeholder engagement, events, reporting, and programme development, contributing to the delivery of high-quality and impactful democratic governance interventions in close collaboration with the Project Manager, Ukraine. Duties and Responsibilities Project Coordination and Implementation Coordinates and ensures timely implementation of democratic governance activities, with a focus on electoral processes, parliamentary strengthening, and constitutional reform; Coordinates day-to-day implementation of project activities, including planning, scheduling, and follow-up on deliverables and milestones; Manages the contracting of consultants and service providers, including drafting TORs, reviewing deliverables, and ensuring compliance with quality and procurement standards; Coordinates with internal teams and technical advisors to ensure coherence and consistency across activities and alignment with international standards and comparative practices. Stakeholder Liaison and Representation Maintains regular communication and operational coordination with key national and international stakeholders, including electoral bodies, ministries, parliaments, civil society, and international partners; Prepares and delivers brief thematic presentations or updates during meetings, roundtables, and consultations as needed; Represents International IDEA at technical-level coordination platforms, including working groups, donor meetings, and stakeholder dialogues, as delegated by the supervisor. Project Development and Budgeting Contributes to the design of new initiatives, including drafting concept notes, identifying relevant interventions, and supporting the development of budgets; Supports donor reporting and proposal writing in collaboration with programme and finance teams; Implement programme activities in accordance with budget. Event and Mission Coordination Leads the substantive and logistical preparation of project-related events, consultative visits, stakeholder meetings, roundtables, and study visits; Oversees coordination with participants, speakers, venues, and logistics providers, ensuring smooth and professional delivery of events; Supports documentation of outcomes and preparation of summary reports from project activities. Monitoring, Evaluation, and Reporting Tracks the progress of activities against work plans, outputs, and indicators; Gathers inputs for internal and donor reporting, including drafting narrative sections of progress and final reports; Collects and systematizes evidence and feedback from consultations, missions, and workshops to inform outcome assessments and learning; Supports regular updates on the political and policy context affecting project delivery. Institutional Engagement and Knowledge Sharing Supports strategic engagement with EU institutions and international democracy support actors to advance programme objectives; Maintains up-to-date knowledge of trends in democratic governance and institutional reforms in Ukraine and the region; Coordinates inputs for knowledge products (e.g., lessons-learned papers, thematic briefs), ensuring the dissemination of project findings and good practices. General Profile Combines a good knowledge of the state of democracy and governance in Ukraine and the broader region, acquired through academic studies in political science, law, social science or related fields, and the relevant work experience; Will contribute significantly to assigned projects all along the project life cycle, i.e. from project design and funding to project assessment; will lead meetings and discussions, supervise publications and review input from external providers; Can work with minimal guidance and supervision; Adds value to team-based activities in their unit; collaborates with other entities of the Institute; Acts as a model or mentor for less experienced colleagues; Can be expected to travel globally to any geographical area involved in their projects; Follows internal procedures to ensure high standards of performance and compliance with Institutional guidelines; Acts in accordance with International IDEA’ s core values; Respect, Integrity and Professionalism; Integrates gender equality, diversity, and social inclusion (GEDSI) considerations into all tasks and activities, in alignment with the Institute’s Core Values and commitment to the GEDSI policy. Reporting line Project Manager - Electoral Preparedness and Integrity, Ukraine Education and Experience University degree in political science, law, international relations, public administration or other related discipline; Minimum of five (5) years of relevant experience in democracy and governance in Ukraine in the field of elections, political participation and representation, anti-corruption, rule of law or constitutionalism; Demonstrated experience working with Ukrainian national and foreign state authorities; Demonstrated experience in results-based report writing; Demonstrated experience in the implementation of projects in the field of democratic governance; Experience in resource mobilization, including conceptualization and formulation of successful funding proposals and knowledge of fundraising methods; Experience of working in an international context considered an advantage; Fluency in Ukrainian is required; Advanced spoken and written English is required. Terms of Contract: Local Post. Fixed-term appointment for one (1) year. Remuneration - USD 3 418 gross per month Please note as this is a local post, International IDEA will bear no costs relating to the relocation of the selected candidate to the duty station. This position is open to those legally authorized to live and work in Ukraine for the duration of the contract. International IDEA is not able to support work permit applications. Use of AI in Recruitment International IDEA places strong emphasis on human input in recruitment. Candidates must disclose any use of AI tools. All application materials, written assessments, and interview responses (as applicable) must be entirely human-generated and reflect the candidate’s own knowledge, skills, and experience. The use of AI to generate content or misrepresent qualifications is not permitted. AI‑powered assistive technologies (such as screen readers or speech‑to‑text tools) may be used for accessibility purposes to ensure equal access to the recruitment process. Applying for this position: Applications should be submitted online no later than 23:59 (CEST) on 29 July 2026. Please note that all applications must be made in English. Selection Process: Evaluation of qualified candidates will include a written test assessment and up to two (2) interviews. Candidates selected to proceed to the next stage of the process will be contacted once all applications have been carefully reviewed. All candidates will be notified of the status of their application via email once the process has been finalized. Due to the anticipated volume of applications, International IDEA will review applications on a rolling basis, and the vacancy may close earlier than the advertised deadline. Applicants are therefore encouraged to apply early. International IDEA is an equal-opportunity employer which seeks to further diversify its staff in terms of gender, culture and nationality.
item.dc:creator: International IDEA

item.title: Project Manager, Electoral Preparedness and Integrity, Ukraine
item.description: Context The International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance (International IDEA) is an intergovernmental organization of 35 member states, with a mission to support sustainable democracy worldwide through policy-relevant knowledge, capacity development, advocacy, and the convening of dialogues. The Institute pursues this mission by developing, sharing and enabling the use of comparative knowledge to reform-makers, combining research, global knowledge tools, and technical assistance to support democratic reform and influence policies and politics at the national and global level. The Regional Europe Programme (REP) has a strategic objective to support democracy in Europe, with an emphasis on Eastern Europe and the Western Balkans. In Ukraine, International IDEA aims to support state and civil society organizations in enhancing the resilience and quality of democratic institutions and processes. Key areas of our engagement relate to electoral processes and political finance, parliamentary strengthening, and promoting the expertise and capacity of civil society organizations to participate in decision-making processes. International IDEA programming in Ukraine has expanded significantly. The Programme’s key areas of focus are provision of support to parliamentary strengthening and to enhancing electoral integrity; other areas of work may include activities to foster greater civic engagement in democratic processes, support to strengthening the culture of constitutionalism, digitalization, promotion of gender equality and women’s participation in political and public life. The Project Manager will lead the design, planning and implementation of assigned projects and activities under International IDEA’s Ukraine programme portfolio, contributing to Ukraine’s democratic resilience, institutional recovery and reform agenda. Based in Kyiv, the incumbent will work as part of a larger Ukraine programme team based in Kyiv and Brussels, in close coordination with other Project Managers and programme staff, and will also work closely with the Central Election Commission of Ukraine, the Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine, and other key national institutions and partners. Duties and Responsibilities Programme Management and Coordination Manages and coordinates assigned projects and activities under International IDEA’s Ukraine programme portfolio, with a focus on one or more thematic areas, including electoral assistance, ICT and cybersecurity, training, voter education, strategic communication, electoral risk management, parliamentary strengthening, constitution-building, political finance, foreign information manipulation and interference, regional development, and EU integration. The specific portfolio will be determined based on programme needs and the relevant experience of the successful candidate; Leads the design, planning, implementation, monitoring and learning of assigned projects and activities, ensuring timely, high-quality and results-oriented delivery; Develops and updates workplans, activity plans, timelines and implementation tools, and ensures effective day-to-day coordination with colleagues based in Kyiv, Brussels and HQ; Monitors relevant political, electoral, parliamentary, reform and socio-economic developments in Ukraine and contributes contextual and technical analysis to inform programme design and adaptive implementation; Identifies and manages operational, political, security and reputational risks related to assigned projects and activities. Project Development and Resource Mobilization Identifies emerging needs and programmatic opportunities in consultation with Ukrainian counterparts, International IDEA colleagues and partners; Contributes to the development of project concepts, concept notes and funding proposals in close collaboration with colleagues in Kyiv, Brussels and HQ; Supports donor engagement, communication and visibility related to assigned projects and contributes to resource mobilization efforts. Partnership Development Maintains and develops partnerships with key stakeholders in Ukraine, including the Central Election Commission, election administration bodies, the Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine, relevant ministries and state institutions, academia, think tanks and civil society organizations; Fosters effective cooperation with International IDEA’s Member States, donor agencies, international organizations and democracy assistance actors based in Kyiv; Co-creates project interventions with national counterparts and partners to ensure relevance, ownership and sustainability. Representation and Thematic Interventions Represents International IDEA in meetings, coordination platforms, technical discussions, public events and high-level forums, as assigned; Delivers thematic presentations, contributes to policy dialogue and ensures International IDEA’s visibility in areas relevant to the assigned portfolio; Prepares thematic briefs, analytical notes, talking points, presentations and other technical inputs to support programme activities and external engagement. Procurement, Consultant and Partner Management Manages and/or coordinates the engagement of external consultants, experts, service providers and implementing partners, ensuring the quality and timely delivery of outputs; Prepares or contributes to terms of reference, technical specifications, procurement documentation and evaluation processes related to assigned activities; Ensures that procurement and service provision are carried out in accordance with International IDEA’s procurement policies and procedures and relevant donor requirements; Provides guidance to designated staff, consultants, interns and partners involved in the implementation of assigned projects and activities. Programme and Budget Planning Leads programme and budget planning for assigned portfolio components and contributes to broader Ukraine programme and regional planning processes; Monitors budget execution, expenditure planning and financial delivery of assigned projects in coordination with relevant finance, budget and programme performance colleagues; Ensures oversight of project funds in accordance with donor agreements, International IDEA regulations and approved workplans. Monitoring, Evaluation, Reporting and Learning Leads monitoring, evaluation, reporting and learning for assigned projects and activities, ensuring alignment with International IDEA’s results-based management system; Tracks progress against outputs, outcomes, indicators and donor requirements, and contributes to programme-related progress reports; Identifies lessons learned, good practices and opportunities for adaptive programming and knowledge sharing across the Ukraine programme. General Profile Combines in-depth knowledge, acquired through post-graduate academic achievements, excellent skills and significant experience in their field; Combines in-depth knowledge in current democratization trends and challenges, especially in Ukraine, as well as in the broader Eastern European region, with a particular focus on democratic governance, electoral processes, parliamentary development, constitutional reforms, FIMI, political finance and democracy assessment frameworks; Manages assigned projects throughout the project life cycle, i.e. from strategic planning and project design, funding and budgeting to project assessment and accountability; leads meetings and discussions, supervises publications and input from external providers; Has contributed to publications on their relevant subject; is recognized as a knowledge resource in their broad professional community; Has the intellectual leadership to integrate innovation into their field of expertise; Fosters team-based activities in their unit; collaborates with other entities of the Institute; Acts as a model and mentor for colleagues; Is expected to travel globally to any geographical area involved in their projects; Follows internal procedures to ensure high standards of performance and compliance with Institutional guidelines; Acts in accordance with International IDEA’ s core values; Respect, Integrity and Professionalism; Integrates gender equality, diversity, and social inclusion (GEDSI) considerations into all tasks and activities, in alignment with the Institute’s Core Values and commitment to the GEDSI policy. Reporting line Ukraine Programme Lead Education and Experience University degree, preferably post graduate in political science, law, international relations, public administration or other related discipline; Minimum of eight (8) years of relevant experience in strengthening democracy and accountability in Ukraine; Experienced in the fields of electoral assistance, parliamentary support, FIMI as well as other rule of law and human rights-related fields in Ukraine; Demonstrated experience in conceptualization, formulation and management of project activities; Experience in fundraising and project design is essential; Demonstrated experience in report writing and reporting under a results-based management (RBM) system; Experience of working in an international context considered an advantage. Fluency in written and spoken English and Ukrainian is required.; Terms of Contract: Local Post. Fixed term appointment for one (1) year. Remuneration - USD 4 570 gross per month Please note as this is a local post, International IDEA will bear no costs relating to the relocation of the selected candidate to the duty station. This position is open to those legally authorized to live and work in Ukraine for the duration of the contract. International IDEA is not able to support work permit applications. Use of AI in Recruitment International IDEA places strong emphasis on human input in recruitment. Candidates must disclose any use of AI tools. All application materials, written assessments, and interview responses (as applicable) must be entirely human-generated and reflect the candidate’s own knowledge, skills, and experience. T
item.dc:creator: International IDEA

item.title: Monitoring, Evaluation, Learning and Reporting Officer, Ukraine
item.description: Context The International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance (International IDEA) is an intergovernmental organization of 35 member states, with a mission to support sustainable democracy worldwide through policy-relevant knowledge, capacity development, advocacy, and the convening of dialogues. The Institute pursues this mission by developing, sharing and enabling the use of comparative knowledge to reform-makers, combining research, global knowledge tools, and technical assistance to support democratic reform and influence policies and politics at the national and global level. The Regional Europe Programme (REP) has a strategic objective to support democracy in Europe, with an emphasis on Eastern Europe and the Western Balkans. In Ukraine, International IDEA aims to support state and civil society organizations in enhancing the resilience and quality of democratic institutions and processes. Key areas of our engagement relate to electoral processes and political finance, parliamentary strengthening, and promoting the expertise and capacity of civil society organizations to participate in decision-making processes. International IDEA programming in Ukraine has expanded significantly. The Programme’s key areas of focus are provision of support to parliamentary strengthening and to enhancing electoral integrity; other areas of work may include activities to foster greater civic engagement in democratic processes, support to strengthening the culture of constitutionalism, digitalization, promotion of gender equality and women’s participation in political and public life. The Monitoring, Evaluation, Learning and Reporting Officer will provide monitoring, evaluation and learning expertise, gender and inclusion advisory support, ensure compliance of project activities with contractual requirements, and contribute to institutional cooperation processes with state authorities. The incumbent will provide subject matter expertise and technical oversight to ensure successful implementation of multiple Ukraine activities, and will work in close collaboration with Stockholm-based MEL officer and with Ukraine-based project teams. Duties and Responsibilities Monitoring, Evaluation, Learning and Reporting Supports the design, operationalization and implementation of monitoring, evaluation, learning and reporting systems across programmes and projects, ensuring alignment with organizational policies, donor requirements and institutional standards; Develops and advises on theories of change, results frameworks, indicators, performance measurement plans and reporting frameworks, including the integration of gender-sensitive, inclusive and disaggregated indicators; Provides guidance on data collection methodologies, monitoring tools, reporting templates and evaluation approaches to ensure data quality, ethical standards, consistency and usability for decision-making and accountability; Conducts and coordinates data collection, validation, analysis and documentation, and ensures that monitoring data and evidence are systematically maintained in a timely, accurate and audit-ready manner; Prepares narrative reports to donors and coordinates the preparation of financial reports to donors, ensuring consistency, quality and compliance with donor and institutional requirements; Supports the preparation of periodic, annual and final programme and project reports by consolidating evidence on outputs, outcomes, lessons learned, challenges and adaptive measures; Leads or supports analysis, reporting and learning processes, using monitoring and evaluation findings to inform adaptive management, programme improvement, strategic decision-making and accountability; Supports programme teams on the integration of gender-responsive, intersectional and results-based approaches across the full programme cycle, including design, implementation, monitoring, evaluation, learning and reporting; Serves as a senior subject-matter expert, providing technical leadership, quality assurance and strategic guidance to support effective implementation, evidence-based decision-making and strong results reporting. Capacity Development and Technical Support Provides technical guidance, capacity-building and support to programme staff, consultants and partners; Strengthens the capacity of programme teams and partners to collect, manage, analyze and report data in line with agreed indicators, donor requirements and organizational standards; Contributes to the development and delivery of activity assessment and knowledge products related to monitoring, evaluation, learning, reporting and results-based management; Supports internal coordination by providing technical expertise to planning, decision-making and adaptive management processes, including the integration of gender considerations. Institutional Liaison and Stakeholder Engagement Works closely with state institutions, electoral bodies, civil society organizations and other stakeholders to design, implement and strengthen gender-responsive and results-oriented monitoring, evaluation, learning and reporting frameworks for democratic processes; Supports effective collaboration with public authorities and partners by generating evidence-based, gender-sensitive findings, lessons learned and recommendations to inform policy development, programme design and operational decision-making; Leads and contributes to the collection, analysis and use of sex-disaggregated and inclusion-focused data to assess progress on gender equality, women’s political empowerment and inclusive governance outcomes; Represents International IDEA in meetings, consultations and working groups related to monitoring, evaluation, learning, reporting and results-based management, ensuring that gender equality and inclusion are systematically integrated. General Profile In-depth knowledge and experience of monitoring, evaluation and learning systems, techniques and practices, including outcome mapping tools and processes; Adds value to team-based activities in their team; collaborates with other entities of the Institute as required; builds strong relationships with colleagues and project partners, focuses on results; Can work with minimal guidance and supervision; Acts as a model or mentor for less experienced colleagues; Can be expected to travel to any geographical area involved in their projects; Follows internal procedures to ensure high standards of performance and compliance with Institutional guidelines; Acts in accordance with International IDEA’ s core values; Respect, Integrity and Professionalism; Integrates gender equality, diversity, and social inclusion (GEDSI) considerations into all tasks and activities, in alignment with the Institute’s Core Values and commitment to the GEDSI policy. Reporting line Ukraine Programme Lead Education and Experience University degree in development studies, business administration; public policy, international development or other relevant discipline; Advanced certification in Monitoring & Evaluation, project management, or statistics is desirable; Minimum of five (5) years professional experience in monitoring and evaluation, ideally reporting for donor audiences, for complex and multi-faceted international development projects and programmes, preferably with a focus on support to democratic institutions and civil society organizations; Knowledge and experience of evaluation approaches and methodologies; Demonstrated experience in gender review and support of project management processes; Experience of working in an international context, e.g. in an intergovernmental organization; Experience in networking and communicating with different stakeholders such as government, public institutions, electoral bodies, UN, NGOs and other organizations is considered an advantage. Fluency in written and spoken English and Ukrainian is required.; Terms of Contract: Local Post. Fixed term appointment until 31 December, 2027 Remuneration - USD 3 418 gross per month Please note as this is a local post, International IDEA will bear no costs relating to the relocation of the selected candidate to the duty station. This position is open to those legally authorized to live and work in Ukraine for the duration of the contract. International IDEA is not able to support work permit applications. Use of AI in Recruitment International IDEA places strong emphasis on human input in recruitment. Candidates must disclose any use of AI tools. All application materials, written assessments, and interview responses (as applicable) must be entirely human-generated and reflect the candidate’s own knowledge, skills, and experience. The use of AI to generate content or misrepresent qualifications is not permitted. AI‑powered assistive technologies (such as screen readers or speech‑to‑text tools) may be used for accessibility purposes to ensure equal access to the recruitment process. Applying for this position: Applications should be submitted online no later than 23:59 (CEST) 22 July, 2026. Please note that all applications must be made in English. Selection Process: Evaluation of qualified candidates will include a written test assessment and up to two (2) interviews. Candidates selected to proceed to the next stage of the process will be contacted once all applications have been carefully reviewed. All candidates will be notified of the status of their application via email once the process has been finalized. Due to the anticipated volume of applications, International IDEA will review applications on a rolling basis, and the vacancy may close earlier than the advertised deadline. Applicants are therefore encouraged to apply early. International IDEA is an equal-opportunity employer which seeks to further diversify its staff in terms of gender, culture and nationality.
item.dc:creator: International IDEA

item.title: Administrative Assistant, CSO Bridge - Nigeria (Abuja)
item.description: Context International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance (IDEA) is an intergovernmental organization with 35 member states hailing from different continents around the world. International IDEA aims to support sustainable democracy worldwide, doing so, inter alia, by working with both new and long-established democracies to strengthen the institutions and culture of democracy. Located at the interface of research and practice, International IDEA serves as a forum for dialogue and uses comparative experience and analysis to identify good practices for democracy-building. It also produces knowledge tools and practical guides for democracy support. The Institute maintains a global network of experts, provides training materials, and offers strategic advice to democracy actors at the international, regional, and national levels. The Institute is headquartered in Stockholm with field offices in Africa and West Asia, Asia and the Pacific, and Latin America and the Caribbean. The Civil Society Organizations (CSO) Strengthening Bridge (CSO Bridge) Project is designed to contribute to a more enabling environment for CSOs to thrive and contribute to sustainable development in Nigeria. It will build on the gains made by the recently concluded European Union (EU) funded Agents for Citizen-driven Transformation (ACT) Programme. The programme is funded by the European Union (EU) for a period of three years from 2025 to 2028. Its implementation is expected to result in the following outcomes: Outcome 1: Functioning and effective coordination mechanisms that encourage and maintain an enabling environment for CSO operations are in place at national and sub-national levels established. Outcome 2: Improved capacities amongst CSOs to understand and comply with extant regulatory requirements and apply self-regulatory principles and practices. Outcome 3: Improved regulatory frameworks and practices affecting the formation and operations of CSOs at national and sub-national levels. The Administrative Assistant provides sound logistical and administrative support to the CSO Bridge Project that enables the project to achieve their outcomes and targets, in close coordination with the other projects implemented by IDEA in Nigeria and in collaboration with International IDEA’s Africa and West Asia Regional Office. Duties and Responsibilities 1. Handles administrative requests and queries for the team and as directed by the Procurement and Operations Officer and Head of Programme; 2. Provides timely data on costs (hotel accommodation, venue and transport) to the programme team to support preparation of budgets and analysis; 3. Organizes and coordinates logistical arrangements for meetings, conferences and workshops (obtaining quotes and booking venues, liaising with attendees, etc.); Assists with the preparation of meeting materials and manage printing requests; 4. Handles all travel arrangements (hotel accommodation, transport etc.) for staff and consultants as requested, and raises the relevant purchase requisitions; 5. Seeks feedback from visitors and staff to ensure quality control of travel arrangements (hotels, venue, transport etc.); 6. Develops and updates the contact details of service providers (hotels, ticketing agents, venues etc.) used by the programme (including immigration and the National Planning Commission); 7. Leads protocol arrangements for the programme; Build and maintains effective relationships with key individuals who facilitate the official status of expatriate staff in country; 8. Provides visa application and processing support to programme staff on official duties; Provide relevant visa letters and liaises with relevant embassies and visa agents to obtain required visas; 9. Supports all administrative processes affecting the arrival, presence and exit of project staff (internal and external); Receipt and dispatch the personal effects of international staff on arrival and departure; 10. Maintains knowledge of key processes and official organizations applicable to incoming international staff including immigration, National Planning Commission (NPC) residence registration, driving licenses, etc. 11. Is responsible for documenting appropriate procedures and processes for entry and exit processes, including the various required permits for visiting International IDEA staff and consultants; 12. Provides support to the Procurement and Operations Officer around office inventories, managing the asset register, procurement, office and residential issues etc., including maintenance issues required at the office or residences; 13. Manages the schedule of the office drivers including vehicle maintenance and ensuring that the drivers complete and record details of all the vehicle safety checks; 14. Ensures regular fire drills are completed, fire equipment is maintained and that any security issues are reported promptly to the management team; 15. Provides administrative support to the Head of Programme, including arranging regular internal staff meetings at the Country Office and produces draft minutes and reports of such meetings; 16. Assists with internal and external communication and correspondence of the Head of Programme; Drafts reports, letters, and other relevant correspondence and ensures appropriate filing; 17. Manages the calendar of the Head of Programme and serves as a point of contact for external visitors to the office of the Head of Programme. This includes planning trips for visitors; 18. Carries out other duties and responsibilities as related to the post that may be assigned from time to time. General Profile Has developed knowledge and specialized skills through formal training or work experience and considerable on-the-job experience; Adds considerable value to team-based activities in their unit, and likely to act as an informal resource for colleagues; Collaborates with other entities of the Institute as required; Follows internal procedures to ensure high standards of performance and compliance with Institutional guidelines; Acts in accordance with International IDEA’s Core Values; Respect, Integrity, and Professionalism; Integrates gender equality, diversity, and social inclusion (GEDSI) considerations into all tasks and activities, in alignment with the Institute’s Core Values and commitment to the GEDSI policy. Reporting line Procurement and Operations Officer, (NG-FC). Education and Experience Ordinary diploma (OND) or equivalent and preferably business and/or secretarial education; University degree in business, or other relevant discipline considered an advantage; Minimum of three (3) years’ professional experience in administration, logistics support, procurement and/or business administration; Knowledge and understanding of issues affecting civil societies regulatory environment is beneficial; Experience in networking and communicating with different stakeholders such as government, EU, NGOs, vendors, partners and other organizations is considered an advantage; Experience of working in an international context, e.g., in an NGO, an intergovernmental organization, considered an advantage. Terms of Contract: Local Post. Fixed term appointment till 28 February 2028. Remuneration - USD 990 Monthly Gross Salary Please note as this is a local post, International IDEA will bear no costs relating to the relocation of the selected candidate to the duty station. This position is open to those legally authorized to live and work in Nigeria for the duration of the contract. International IDEA is not able to support work permit applications. Use of AI in Recruitment International IDEA places strong emphasis on human input in recruitment. Candidates must disclose any use of AI tools. All application materials, written assessments, and interview responses (as applicable) must be entirely human-generated and reflect the candidate’s own knowledge, skills, and experience. The use of AI to generate content or misrepresent qualifications is not permitted. AI‑powered assistive technologies (such as screen readers or speech‑to‑text tools) may be used for accessibility purposes to ensure equal access to the recruitment process. Applying for this position: Applications should be submitted online no later than 23:59 (CEST) Sunday 19 July 2026. Please note that all applications must be made in English. Selection Process: Evaluation of qualified candidates will include a written test assessment and up to two (2) interviews. Candidates selected to proceed to the next stage of the process will be contacted once all applications have been carefully reviewed. All candidates will be notified of the status of their application via email once the process has been finalized. Due to the anticipated volume of applications, International IDEA will review applications on a rolling basis, and the vacancy may close earlier than the advertised deadline. Applicants are therefore encouraged to apply early. International IDEA is an equal-opportunity employer which seeks to further diversify its staff in terms of gender, culture and nationality.
item.dc:creator: International IDEA
